4 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 9. (a) If the tax levied under section
5 6 of this chapter is increased by an ordinance of the county fiscal body,
6 the county executive shall create a commission to promote:
7 (1) economic development; and
8 (2) the development and growth of the convention, visitor, and
9 tourism industry;
10 in the county.
11 (b) The composition and appointment of the membership of a
12 commission created under subsection (a) must be as follows:
13 (1) Subject to subdivision (2), the county executive shall
14 determine the number of members of the commission. However,
15 the commission also consists of any members appointed under
16 subsection (h).
17 (2) The commission must be composed of an odd number of
18 members.
19 (3) A simple majority of the members must be:
20 (A) engaged in the convention or tourism business;
21 (B) involved in or promoting conventions, visitors, or tourism;
22 or
23 (C) involved in promoting economic development in the
24 county.
25 (4) At least two (2) members must be engaged in the business of
26 renting or furnishing rooms, lodging, or accommodations (as
27 described in section 6 of this chapter) if at least two (2) such
28 individuals are available and willing to serve on the commission.
29 (5) Not more than a simple majority of the members may be
30 affiliated with the same political party.
31 (6) Each member must reside in the county.
32 (7) The executive of the largest municipality of the county shall
33 appoint a number of members equal to:
34 (A) the total number of members of the commission;
35 multiplied by
36 (B) a fraction:
37 (i) the numerator of which is equal to the population of the
38 largest municipality in the county; and
39 (ii) the denominator of which is equal to the total population
40 of the county;
41 rounded to the nearest whole number. The county executive shall
42 determine who appoints the members of the commission not
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

190
1 appointed by the executive of the largest municipality of the
2 county.
3 (c) All terms of office of commission members begin on January 1.
4 Initial appointments must be for staggered terms, with subsequent
5 appointments for two (2) year terms. A member whose term expires
6 may be reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs, the
7 appointing authority shall appoint a qualified person to serve for the
8 remainder of the term. If an initial appointment is not made by
9 February 1 or a vacancy is not filled within thirty (30) days after the
10 vacancy occurs, the commission shall appoint a member by majority
11 vote.
12 (d) A member of the commission may be removed for cause by the
13 member's appointing authority.
14 (e) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
15 commission members are entitled to reimbursement for necessary
16 expenses incurred in the performance of their respective duties.
17 (f) Each commission member, before entering the member's duties,
18 shall take an oath of office in the usual form, to be endorsed upon the
19 member's certificate of appointment and promptly filed with the clerk
20 of the circuit court of the county.
21 (g) The commission shall meet after January 1 each year for the
22 purpose of organization. The commission shall elect one (1) of its
23 members president, another vice president, another secretary, and
24 another treasurer. The members elected to those offices shall perform
25 the duties pertaining to the offices. The first officers chosen shall serve
26 from the date of their election until their successors are elected and
27 qualified. A majority of the commission constitutes a quorum, and the
28 concurrence of a majority of the commission is necessary to authorize
29 any action.
30 (h) The commission must also contain a member appointed by
31 the city executive of each city within the county (other than the
32 municipality described in subsection (b)(7)).
33 SECTION 167. IC 6-9-11-2 IS AMENDED TO READ AS
34 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 2. (a) If a tax is levied
35 under section 6(a) of this chapter, there is created a five (5) member
36 convention and visitor commission (referred to as the "commission" in
37 this chapter) plus any additional members appointed under
38 subsection (e), whose purpose it is to promote the development and
39 growth of the convention and visitor industry in the county.
40 (b) The county council, by majority vote, shall appoint two (2)
41 members of the commission, one (1) of whom must be engaged in the
42 hotel or motel business in the county and one (1) of whom must be
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

191
1 engaged in the profession of education within the county. The two (2)
2 members appointed by the county council may not be members of the
3 same political party. The county commissioners, by majority vote, shall
4 appoint two (2) members of the commission, one (1) of whom must be
5 engaged in the hotel or motel business within the county and one (1) of
6 whom must be representative of business, industry or labor within the
7 county. The two (2) members appointed by the county commissioners
8 may not be members of the same political party. The mayor of the
9 largest city in the county, according to the preceding decennial United
10 States census, shall appoint one (1) member of the commission. The
11 mayor's appointee must be engaged in the hotel or motel business in the
12 county.
13 (c) The initial terms of office of the members of the commission
14 begin on the date a tax is levied under section 6(a) of this chapter. The
15 initial terms of the members appointed by the county commissioners
16 end on December 31 of the year in which the tax is levied, and the
17 initial terms of the members appointed by the county council and by the
18 mayor of the largest city end on December 31 of the immediately
19 following year. All terms of office after the initial terms begin on
20 January 1 and end on December 31. After the initial terms, members of
21 the commission appointed by the county council and by the mayor of
22 the largest city serve two (2) year terms, and members appointed by the
23 county commissioners serve one (1) year terms. A member whose term
24 expires may be reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs,
25 the commission, by majority vote shall, within thirty (30) days, appoint
26 a qualified person to serve the remainder of the term. If the commission
27 fails to appoint a person within thirty (30) days of the vacancy, the
28 original appointing official or body for that vacant position, by majority
29 vote, shall appoint a qualified person to serve the remainder of the
30 term.
31 (d) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
32 commission members shall receive reimbursement for necessary
33 expenses, but only when the necessary expenses are incurred in the
34 performance of their respective duties.
35 (e) The commission must also contain a member appointed by
36 the city executive of each city within the county (other than the city
37 described in subsection (b)).

29 (1) hotel;

11 eight percent (8%) in DeKalb County; or
12 (5) subject to subsection (g), after June 30, 2026, the rate of
13 eight percent (8%) in Noble County.
14 The tax is imposed on the gross retail income derived from lodging
15 income only and is in addition to the state gross retail tax imposed
16 under IC 6-2.5.
17 (d) The county fiscal body may adopt an ordinance to require that
18 the tax shall be paid monthly to the county treasurer. If such an
19 ordinance is adopted, the tax shall be paid to the county treasurer not
20 more than twenty (20) days after the end of the month the tax is
21 collected. If such an ordinance is not adopted, the tax shall be imposed,
22 paid, and collected in exactly the same manner as the state gross retail
23 tax is imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5.

25 liabilities, procedures, penalties, definitions, exemptions, and
26 administration are applicable to the imposition and administration of

28 are in conflict or inconsistent with the specific provisions of this
29 chapter or the requirements of the county treasurer. If the tax is paid to
30 the department of state revenue, the return to be filed for the payment
31 of the tax under this section may be either a separate return or may be
32 combined with the return filed for the payment of the state gross retail
33 tax as the department of state revenue may, by rule, determine.

1 number of members to the commission that the executive of the largest

9 initial appointment is not made by February 1 or a vacancy is not filled

EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

205
1 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the county
2 may impose a tax on every person engaged in the business of renting
3 or furnishing, for periods of less than thirty (30) days, any room or
4 rooms, lodgings, or accommodations in any:
5 (1) hotel;
6 (2) motel;
7 (3) boat motel;
8 (4) inn;
9 (5) college or university memorial union;
10 (6) college or university residence hall or dormitory; or
11 (7) tourist cabin;
12 located in the county.
13 (b) The tax does not apply to gross income received in a transaction
14 in which:
15 (1) a student rents lodgings in a college or university residence
16 hall while that student participates in a course of study for which
17 the student receives college credit from a college or university
18 located in the county; or
19 (2) a person rents a room, lodging, or accommodations for a
20 period of thirty (30) days or more.
21 (c) The following apply to the tax rate imposed under this section:
22 (1) Before July 1, 2023, the tax may not exceed the rate of five
23 percent (5%) on the gross retail income derived from lodging
24 income only and is in addition to the state gross retail tax imposed
25 under IC 6-2.5.
26 (2) After June 30, 2023, and before January 1, 2049, the tax
27 may not exceed the rate of eight percent (8%) on the gross retail
28 income derived from lodging income only and is in addition to the
29 state gross retail tax imposed under IC 6-2.5.
30 (3) After December 31, 2048, a tax rate imposed under this
31 chapter may not exceed five percent (5%). The portion of the
32 tax rate imposed under subsection (2) that exceeds five
33 percent (5%) shall expire January 1, 2049.
34 (d) The county fiscal body may adopt an ordinance to require that
35 the tax shall be paid monthly to the county treasurer. If such an
36 ordinance is adopted, the tax shall be paid to the county treasurer not
37 more than twenty (20) days after the end of the month the tax is
38 collected. If such an ordinance is not adopted, the tax shall be imposed,
39 paid, and collected in exactly the same manner as the state gross retail
40 tax is imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5.
41 (e) All of the provisions of IC 6-2.5 relating to rights, duties,
42 liabilities, procedures, penalties, definitions, exemptions, and

37 section, "fund" refers to the convention, visitor, tourism
38 promotion, and capital fund established under section 4(a)(2) of

EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

209
1 (c) Money in the fund shall be expended only as provided in
2 section 4(c) of this chapter.
3 (d) The commission may transfer money in the fund to any
4 Indiana nonprofit corporation for the purpose of promotion and
5 encouragement in the county of conventions, trade shows, visitors,
6 or special events. The commission may transfer money under this
7 subsection only after approving the transfer. The commission may
8 transfer money under this subsection on a monthly basis or at
9 another frequency as determined by the commission.
10 (e) The commission must approve any transfer of money from
11 the fund and may transfer money from the fund to support capital
12 projects in the county that promote long term tourism, convention,
13 or recreation projects proposed by any of the following:
14 (1) The county government.
15 (2) A separate body corporate and politic in Hamilton County.
16 (3) Any Indiana nonprofit corporation in Hamilton County.
17 The commission may transfer money under this subsection on a
18 monthly basis or at another frequency as determined by the
19 commission.
20 (f) The commission may also review and approve proposals
21 submitted by applicants that seek money from the fund with the
22 purpose and view of enhancing or providing support for capital
23 projects that promote long term tourism, convention, or other
24 economic development related to recreation. Funding available
25 under this subsection shall be made available on an annual basis.
26 In determining whether to provide funding to a particular capital
27 project under this subsection, the commission may use the
28 following factors as a guide for capital project funding:
29 (1) The proposed capital project is believed to be economically
30 sound to the Hamilton County tourism, convention, or
31 recreation economy and is also believed to be beneficial to:
32 (A) the general population of Hamilton County; or
33 (B) a particular location in Hamilton County.
34 (2) The proposed capital project provides for reasonably
35 adequate public assembly, gathering, or entertainment space
36 and is integrally related to enhancing the tourism, convention,
37 or recreation opportunities in Hamilton County or a
38 particular location in Hamilton County.
39 (3) The commission makes a reasonable effort to assess
40 whether a proposed capital project aligns with the purpose of
41 the commission and has a direct, indirect, or supportive
42 relationship to the mission and promotional efforts of the
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

210
1 commission as established and funded by the fund.
2 Any remaining funds collected that are not awarded during an
3 application period revert to the fund and may be used for
4 distribution in a subsequent application period.
5 (g) An applicant that receives a grant of money from the fund
6 under subsection (f):
7 (1) must agree to provide to the commission proof of project
8 completion, including proof that the project was completed
9 through the use of the grant money; and
10 (2) may be subject to annual financial reporting and audit.

21 (b) The county executive shall determine the number of members,
22 which must be an odd number, to be appointed to the commission. The
23 commission must also include any additional members appointed
24 under subsection (h). A simple majority of the members must be:
25 (1) engaged in a convention, visitor, or tourism business; or
26 (2) involved in or promoting conventions, visitors, or tourism.
27 A member appointed to the commission under subdivision (1) or (2)
28 need not be a resident of the county if the member is an owner or an
29 executive level employee of a convention, visitor, or tourism business
30 that is located within the county. However, the member must be a
31 resident of Indiana. If available and willing to serve, at least two (2) of
32 the members must be engaged in the business of renting or furnishing
33 rooms, lodging, or accommodations (as described in section 3 of this
34 chapter). Not more than one (1) member may be affiliated with the
35 same business entity. Except as otherwise provided in this subsection,
36 each member must reside in the county. The county executive shall
37 also determine who will make the appointments to the commission,
38 except that the executive of the largest municipality in the county shall
39 appoint a number of the members of the commission, which number
40 shall be in the same ratio to the total size of the commission (rounded
41 off to the nearest whole number) that the population of the largest
42 municipality bears to the total population of the county.
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

214
1 (c) All terms of office of commission members begin on January 1.
2 Initial appointments must be for staggered terms, with subsequent
3 appointments for two (2) year terms. A member whose term expires
4 may be reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs, the
5 appointing authority shall appoint a qualified person to serve for the
6 remainder of the term. If an initial appointment is not made by
7 February 1 or a vacancy is not filled within thirty (30) days, the
8 commission shall appoint a member by majority vote.
9 (d) A member of the commission may be removed for cause by the
10 member's appointing authority.
11 (e) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
12 commission members are entitled to reimbursement for necessary
13 expenses incurred in the performance of their respective duties.
14 (f) Each commission member, before entering the member's duties,
15 shall take an oath of office in the usual form, to be endorsed upon the
16 member's certificate of appointment and promptly filed with the clerk
17 of the circuit court of the county.
18 (g) The commission shall meet after January 1 each year for the
19 purpose of organization. It shall elect one (1) of its members president,
20 another vice president, another secretary, and another treasurer. The
21 members elected to those offices shall perform the duties pertaining to
22 the offices. The first officers chosen shall serve from the date of their
23 election until their successors are elected and qualified. A majority of
24 the commission constitutes a quorum, and the concurrence of a
25 majority of the commission is necessary to authorize any action.
26 (h) The commission must also include a member appointed by
27 the city executive of each city within the county (other than a city
28 described in subsection (b)).
29 SECTION 190. IC 6-9-75-6, AS ADDED BY P.L.230-2025,
30 SECTION 124, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS
31 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 6. (a) The county executive shall

35 counties shall enter into an agreement under IC 36-1-7.
36 (b) The county executive shall determine the number of members,
37 which must be an odd number, to be appointed to the commission. The
38 commission must also include any additional members appointed
39 under subsection (h). Each of the members must be:
40 (1) engaged in a convention, visitor, or tourism business; or
41 (2) involved in or promoting conventions, visitors, or tourism.
42 A member who is an owner or an executive level employee of a
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

6 (2) of the members must be engaged in the business of renting or
7 furnishing rooms, lodging, or accommodations (as described in section
8 3 of this chapter). The county executive shall also determine who will
9 make the appointments to the commission.

33 the commission constitutes a quorum, and the concurrence of a

3 SECTION 192. IC 6-9-78.1 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
4 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
5 JULY 1, 2026]:
6 Chapter 78.1. Lagro Food and Beverage Tax
7 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to the town of Lagro.
8 Sec. 2. The definitions in IC 6-9-12-1 apply throughout this
9 chapter.
10 Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the town may adopt an ordinance
11 to impose an excise tax, known as the town food and beverage tax,
12 on transactions described in section 4 of this chapter. The fiscal
13 body of the town may adopt an ordinance under this subsection
14 only after the town fiscal body has previously:
15 (1) adopted a resolution in support of the proposed town food
16 and beverage tax; and
17 (2) held at least one (1) separate public hearing in which a
18 discussion of the proposed ordinance to impose the town food
19 and beverage tax is the only substantive issue on the agenda
20 for the public hearing.
21 (b) If the town fiscal body adopts an ordinance under subsection
22 (a), the town fiscal body shall immediately send a certified copy of
23 the ordinance to the department of state revenue.
24 (c) If the town fiscal body adopts an ordinance under subsection
25 (a), the town food and beverage tax applies to transactions that
26 occur after the last day of the month following the month in which
27 the ordinance is adopted.
28 Sec. 4. (a) Except as provided in subsection (c), a tax imposed
29 under section 3 of this chapter applies to a transaction in which
30 food or beverage is furnished, prepared, or served:
31 (1) for consumption at a location or on equipment provided by
32 a retail merchant;
33 (2) in the town; and
34 (3) by a retail merchant for consideration.
35 (b) Transactions described in subsection (a)(1) include
36 transactions in which food or beverage is:
37 (1) served by a retail merchant off the merchant's premises;
38 (2) food sold in a heated state or heated by a retail merchant;
39 (3) made of two (2) or more food ingredients, mixed or
40 combined by a retail merchant for sale as a single item (other
41 than food that is only cut, repackaged, or pasteurized by the
42 seller, and eggs, fish, meat, poultry, and foods containing these
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

218
1 raw animal foods requiring cooking by the consumer as
2 recommended by the federal Food and Drug Administration
3 in chapter 3, subpart 3-401.11 of its Food Code so as to
4 prevent food borne illnesses); or
5 (4) food sold with eating utensils provided by a retail
6 merchant, including plates, knives, forks, spoons, glasses,
7 cups, napkins, or straws (for purposes of this subdivision, a
8 plate does not include a container or package used to
9 transport the food).
10 (c) The town food and beverage tax does not apply to the
11 furnishing, preparing, or serving of a food or beverage in a
12 transaction that is exempt, or to the extent the transaction is
13 exempt, from the state gross retail tax imposed by IC 6-2.5.
14 Sec. 5. The town food and beverage tax rate:
15 (1) must be imposed in an increment of twenty-five
16 hundredths percent (0.25%); and
17 (2) may not exceed one percent (1%);
18 of the gross retail income received by the merchant from the food
19 or beverage transaction described in section 4 of this chapter. For
20 purposes of this chapter, the gross retail income received by the
21 retail merchant from a transaction does not include the amount of
22 tax imposed on the transaction under IC 6-2.5.
23 Sec. 6. A tax imposed under this chapter shall be imposed, paid,
24 and collected in the same manner that the state gross retail tax is
25 imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5. However, the return
26 to be filed with the payment of the tax imposed under this chapter
27 may be made on a separate return or may be combined with the
28 return filed for the payment of the state gross retail tax, as
29 prescribed by the department of state revenue.
30 Sec. 7. The amounts received from the tax imposed under this
31 chapter shall be paid monthly by the treasurer of state to the town
32 fiscal officer upon warrants issued by the state comptroller.
33 Sec. 8. (a) If a tax is imposed under section 3 of this chapter by
34 the town, the town fiscal officer shall establish a food and beverage
35 tax receipts fund.
36 (b) The town fiscal officer shall deposit in the fund all amounts
37 received under this chapter.
38 (c) Money earned from the investment of money in the fund
39 becomes a part of the fund.
40 Sec. 9. Money in the food and beverage tax receipts fund must
41 be used by the town only for the following purposes:
42 (1) For economic development purposes, including the pledge
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

219
1 of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases, or other
2 obligations for economic development purposes.
3 (2) For park and recreation purposes, including the purchase
4 of land for park and recreation purposes.
5 (3) The pledge of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases,
6 or other obligations incurred for a purpose described in
7 subdivision (2).
8 Sec. 10. With respect to obligations for which a pledge has been
9 made under section 9 of this chapter, the general assembly
10 covenants with the holders of the obligations that this chapter will
11 not be repealed or amended in a manner that will adversely affect
12 the imposition or collection of the tax imposed under this chapter
13 if the payment of any of the obligations is outstanding.
14 Sec. 11. (a) If the town imposes the tax authorized by this
15 chapter, the tax terminates on January 1, 2049.
16 (b) This chapter expires January 1, 2049.
17 SECTION 193. IC 6-9-78.2 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
18 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
19 UPON PASSAGE]:
20 Chapter 78.2. Rush County Food and Beverage Tax
21 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to Rush County.
22 Sec. 2. The definitions in IC 6-9-12-1 apply throughout this
23 chapter.
24 Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the county may adopt an ordinance
25 on or before December 31, 2026, to impose an excise tax, known as
26 the county food and beverage tax, on transactions described in
27 section 4 of this chapter. The fiscal body of the county may adopt
28 an ordinance under this subsection only after the county fiscal
29 body has previously held at least one (1) separate public hearing in
30 which a discussion of the proposed ordinance to impose the county
31 food and beverage tax is the only substantive issue on the agenda
32 for the public hearing.
33 (b) If the county fiscal body adopts an ordinance under
34 subsection (a), the county fiscal body shall immediately send a
35 certified copy of the ordinance to the department of state revenue.
36 (c) If the county fiscal body adopts an ordinance under
37 subsection (a), the county food and beverage tax applies to
38 transactions that occur after the later of the following:
39 (1) The day specified in the ordinance.
40 (2) The last day of the month that succeeds the month in
41 which the ordinance is adopted.
42 Sec. 4. (a) Except as provided in subsection (c), a tax imposed
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

220
1 under section 3 of this chapter applies to a transaction in which

6 (3) by a retail merchant for consideration.
7 (b) Transactions described in subsection (a)(1) include
8 transactions in which food or beverage is:
9 (1) served by a retail merchant off the merchant's premises;
10 (2) sold in a heated state or heated by a retail merchant;
11 (3) made of two (2) or more food ingredients, mixed or
12 combined by a retail merchant for sale as a single item (other
13 than food that is only cut, repackaged, or pasteurized by the
14 seller, and eggs, fish, meat, poultry, and foods containing these
15 raw animal foods requiring cooking by the consumer as
16 recommended by the federal Food and Drug Administration
17 in chapter 3, subpart 3-401.11 of its Food Code so as to
18 prevent food borne illnesses); or
19 (4) sold with eating utensils provided by a retail merchant,
20 including plates, knives, forks, spoons, glasses, cups, napkins,
21 or straws (for purposes of this subdivision, a plate does not
22 include a container or package used to transport food).
23 (c) The county food and beverage tax does not apply to the
24 furnishing, preparing, or serving of a food or beverage in a
25 transaction that is exempt, or to the extent the transaction is
26 exempt, from the state gross retail tax imposed by IC 6-2.5.
27 Sec. 5. The county food and beverage tax rate:
28 (1) must be imposed in an increment of twenty-five
29 hundredths percent (0.25%); and
30 (2) may not exceed one percent (1%);
31 of the gross retail income received by the merchant from the food
32 or beverage transaction described in section 4 of this chapter. For
33 purposes of this chapter, the gross retail income received by the
34 retail merchant from a transaction does not include the amount of
35 tax imposed on the transaction under IC 6-2.5.

37 collected in the same manner that the state gross retail tax is
38 imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5. However, the return
39 to be filed with the payment of the tax imposed under this chapter
40 may be made on a separate return or may be combined with the
41 return filed for the payment of the state gross retail tax, as
42 prescribed by the department of state revenue.

1 Sec. 7. The amounts received from the tax imposed under this
2 chapter shall be paid monthly by the treasurer of state to the
3 county fiscal officer upon warrants issued by the state comptroller.
4 Sec. 8. (a) If a tax is imposed under section 3 of this chapter by
5 the county, the county fiscal officer shall establish a food and
6 beverage tax receipts fund.
7 (b) The county fiscal officer shall deposit in the fund all amounts
8 received under this chapter.
9 (c) Money earned from the investment of money in the fund
10 becomes a part of the fund.
11 Sec. 9. Money in the food and beverage tax receipts fund must
12 be used by the county only for the following purposes:
13 (1) Economic development and tourism related purposes or
14 facilities, including the purchase of land for economic
15 development or tourism related purposes.
16 (2) The pledge of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases,
17 or other obligations incurred for a purpose described in
18 subdivision (1).
19 Revenue derived from the imposition of a tax under this chapter
20 may be treated by the county as additional revenue for the purpose
21 of fixing its budget for the budget year during which the revenues
22 are to be distributed to the county.
23 Sec. 10. With respect to obligations for which a pledge has been
24 made under section 9 of this chapter, the general assembly
25 covenants with the holders of the obligations that this chapter will
26 not be repealed or amended in a manner that will adversely affect
27 the imposition or collection of the tax imposed under this chapter
28 if the payment of any of the obligations is outstanding.
29 Sec. 11. (a) If the county imposes the tax authorized by this
30 chapter, the tax terminates on January 1, 2049.
31 (b) This chapter expires January 1, 2049.

3 SECTION 195. IC 6-9-78.4 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
4 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
5 JULY 1, 2026]:
6 Chapter 78.4. Huntington Food and Beverage Tax
7 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to one (1) but not both:
8 (1) Huntington County; or
9 (2) the city of Huntington.
10 If Huntington County is the first to adopt an ordinance under
11 section 3 of this chapter to impose a food and beverage tax, the city
12 of Huntington is thereafter prohibited from imposing a food and
13 beverage tax under this chapter. If the city of Huntington is the
14 first to adopt an ordinance under section 3 of this chapter to
15 impose a food and beverage tax, Huntington County is thereafter
16 prohibited from imposing a food and beverage tax under this
17 chapter.
18 Sec. 2. (a) The definitions in IC 6-9-12-1 apply throughout this
19 chapter.
20 (b) For purposes of this chapter, "adopting body" means either
21 Huntington County or the city of Huntington, whichever is first to
22 adopt a food and beverage tax under this chapter.
23 Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the adopting body may adopt an
24 ordinance to impose an excise tax, known as a food and beverage
25 tax, on transactions described in section 4 of this chapter. The
26 fiscal body of the adopting body may adopt an ordinance under
27 this subsection only after the fiscal body of the adopting body has
28 previously:
29 (1) adopted a resolution in support of the proposed food and
30 beverage tax; and
31 (2) held at least one (1) separate public hearing in which a
32 discussion of the proposed ordinance to impose the food and
33 beverage tax is the only substantive issue on the agenda for
34 the public hearing.
35 (b) If the fiscal body of the adopting body adopts an ordinance
36 under subsection (a), the fiscal body shall immediately send a
37 certified copy of the ordinance to the department of state revenue.
38 (c) If the fiscal body of the adopting body adopts an ordinance
39 under subsection (a), the food and beverage tax applies to
40 transactions that occur after the last day of the month following
41 the month in which the ordinance is adopted.
42 Sec. 4. (a) Except as provided in subsection (c), a tax imposed
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

225
1 under section 3 of this chapter applies to a transaction in which
2 food or beverage is furnished, prepared, or served:
3 (1) for consumption at a location or on equipment provided by
4 a retail merchant;
5 (2) in the boundary of the adopting body; and
6 (3) by a retail merchant for consideration.
7 (b) Transactions described in subsection (a)(1) include
8 transactions in which food or beverage is:
9 (1) served by a retail merchant off the merchant's premises;
10 (2) food sold in a heated state or heated by a retail merchant;
11 (3) made of two (2) or more food ingredients, mixed or
12 combined by a retail merchant for sale as a single item (other
13 than food that is only cut, repackaged, or pasteurized by the
14 seller, and eggs, fish, meat, poultry, and foods containing these
15 raw animal foods requiring cooking by the consumer as
16 recommended by the federal Food and Drug Administration
17 in chapter 3, subpart 3-401.11 of its Food Code so as to
18 prevent food borne illnesses); or
19 (4) food sold with eating utensils provided by a retail
20 merchant, including plates, knives, forks, spoons, glasses,
21 cups, napkins, or straws (for purposes of this subdivision, a
22 plate does not include a container or package used to
23 transport the food).
24 (c) The food and beverage tax does not apply to the furnishing,
25 preparing, or serving of a food or beverage in a transaction that is
26 exempt, or to the extent the transaction is exempt, from the state
27 gross retail tax imposed by IC 6-2.5.
28 Sec. 5. The food and beverage tax rate:
29 (1) must be imposed in an increment of twenty-five
30 hundredths percent (0.25%); and
31 (2) may not exceed one percent (1%);
32 of the gross retail income received by the merchant from the food
33 or beverage transaction described in section 4 of this chapter. For
34 purposes of this chapter, the gross retail income received by the
35 retail merchant from a transaction does not include the amount of
36 tax imposed on the transaction under IC 6-2.5.
37 Sec. 6. A tax imposed under this chapter shall be imposed, paid,
38 and collected in the same manner that the state gross retail tax is
39 imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5. However, the return
40 to be filed with the payment of the tax imposed under this chapter
41 may be made on a separate return or may be combined with the
42 return filed for the payment of the state gross retail tax, as
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

226
1 prescribed by the department of state revenue.
2 Sec. 7. The amounts received from the tax imposed under this
3 chapter shall be paid monthly by the treasurer of state to the fiscal
4 body of the adopting body upon warrants issued by the state
5 comptroller.
6 Sec. 8. (a) If a tax is imposed under section 3 of this chapter by
7 the adopting body, the fiscal body of the adopting body shall
8 establish a food and beverage tax receipts fund.
9 (b) The fiscal body of the adopting body shall deposit in the fund
10 all amounts received under this chapter.
11 (c) Money earned from the investment of money in the fund
12 becomes a part of the fund.
13 Sec. 9. Money in the food and beverage tax receipts fund must
14 be used by the adopting body only for the following purposes:
15 (1) For economic development purposes, including the pledge
16 of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases, or other
17 obligations for economic development purposes.
18 (2) For park and recreation purposes, including the purchase
19 of land for park and recreation purposes.
20 (3) The pledge of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases,
21 or other obligations incurred for a purpose described in
22 subdivision (2).
23 Sec. 10. With respect to obligations for which a pledge has been
24 made under section 9 of this chapter, the general assembly
25 covenants with the holders of the obligations that this chapter will
26 not be repealed or amended in a manner that will adversely affect
27 the imposition or collection of the tax imposed under this chapter
28 if the payment of any of the obligations is outstanding.
29 Sec. 11. (a) If the adopting body imposes the tax authorized by
30 this chapter, the tax terminates on January 1, 2049.
31 (b) This chapter expires January 1, 2049.

39 the decision to the circuit court, superior court, or probate court of the

41 taken from an action of the board. The cause of action shall be tried de

9 commodity contract, or commodity account.
10 (50) "Jurisdiction of organization", with respect to a registered
11 organization, means the jurisdiction under whose law the
12 organization is formed or organized.
13 (51) "Letter-of-credit right" means a right to payment or
14 performance under a letter of credit, whether or not the
15 beneficiary has demanded or is at the time entitled to demand
16 payment or performance. The term does not include the right of
17 a beneficiary to demand payment or performance under a letter of
18 credit.
19 (52) "Lien creditor" means:
20 (A) a creditor that has acquired a lien on the property involved
21 by attachment, levy, or the like;
22 (B) an assignee for benefit of creditors from the time of
23 assignment;
24 (C) a trustee in bankruptcy from the date of the filing of the
25 petition; or
26 (D) a receiver in equity from the time of appointment.
27 (53) "Manufactured home" means a structure, transportable in one
28 (1) or more sections, which, in the traveling mode, is eight (8)
29 body feet or more in width or forty (40) body feet or more in
30 length, or, when erected on site, is three hundred twenty (320) or
31 more square feet, and which is built on with or without a
32 permanent chassis and designed to be used as a dwelling with or
33 without a permanent foundation when connected to the required
34 utilities, and includes the plumbing, heating, air conditioning, and
35 electrical systems contained therein. The term includes any
36 structure that meets all of the requirements of this subdivision
37 except the size requirements, and with respect to which the
38 manufacturer voluntarily files a certification required by the
39 United States Secretary of Housing and Urban Development and
40 complies with the standards established under Title 42 of the
41 United States Code.
42 (54) The following terms have the following meanings:
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

251
1 (A) "Manufactured-home transaction" means a secured
2 transaction:
3 (i) that creates a purchase-money security interest in a
4 manufactured home, other than a manufactured home held
5 as inventory; or
6 (ii) in which a manufactured home, other than a
7 manufactured home held as inventory, is the primary
8 collateral.
9 (B) "Money" has the meaning set forth in IC 26-1-1-201(24),
10 but does not include a deposit account.
11 (55) "Mortgage" means a consensual interest in real property,
12 including fixtures, that secures payment or performance of an
13 obligation.
14 (56) "New debtor" means a person that becomes bound as debtor
15 under IC 26-1-9.1-203(d) by a security agreement previously
16 entered into by another person.
17 (57) "New value" means (i) money, (ii) money's worth in
18 property, services, or new credit, or (iii) release by a transferee of
19 an interest in property previously transferred to the transferee.
20 The term does not include an obligation substituted for another
21 obligation.
22 (58) "Noncash proceeds" means proceeds other than cash
23 proceeds.
24 (59) "Obligor" means a person that, with respect to an obligation
25 secured by a security interest in or an agricultural lien on the
26 collateral, (i) owes payment or other performance of the
27 obligation, (ii) has provided property other than the collateral to
28 secure payment or other performance of the obligation, or (iii) is
29 otherwise accountable in whole or in part for payment or other
30 performance of the obligation. The term does not include issuers
31 or nominated persons under a letter of credit.
32 (60) "Original debtor", except as used in IC 26-1-9.1-310(c),
33 means a person that, as debtor, entered into a security agreement
34 to which a new debtor has become bound under
35 IC 26-1-9.1-203(d).
36 (61) "Payment intangible" means a general intangible under
37 which the account debtor's principal obligation is a monetary
38 obligation. The term includes a controllable payment intangible.
39 (62) "Person related to", with respect to an individual, means:
40 (A) the spouse of the individual;
41 (B) a brother, brother-in-law, sister, or sister-in-law of the
42 individual;

252
1 (C) an ancestor or lineal descendant of the individual or the
2 individual's spouse; or
3 (D) any other relative, by blood or marriage, of the individual
4 or the individual's spouse who shares the same home with the
5 individual.
6 (63) "Person related to", with respect to an organization, means:
7 (A) a person directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by,
8 or under common control with the organization;
9 (B) an officer or director of, or a person performing similar
10 functions with respect to, the organization;
11 (C) an officer or director of, or a person performing similar
12 functions with respect to, a person described in clause (A);
13 (D) the spouse of an individual described in clause (A), (B), or
14 (C); or
15 (E) an individual who is related by blood or marriage to an
16 individual described in clause (A), (B), (C), or (D) and shares
17 the same home with the individual.
18 (64) "Proceeds", except as used in IC 26-1-9.1-609(b), means the
19 following property:
20 (A) Whatever is acquired upon the sale, lease, license,
21 exchange, or other disposition of collateral.
22 (B) Whatever is collected on, or distributed on account of,
23 collateral.
24 (C) Rights arising out of collateral.
25 (D) To the extent of the value of collateral, claims arising out
26 of the loss, nonconformity, or interference with the use of,
27 defects or infringement of rights in, or damage to, the

30 payable to the debtor or the secured party, insurance payable
31 by reason of the loss or nonconformity of, defects or
32 infringement of rights in, or damage to, the collateral.
33 (65) "Promissory note" means an instrument that evidences a
34 promise to pay a monetary obligation, does not evidence an order
35 to pay, and does not contain an acknowledgment by a bank that
36 the bank has received for deposit a sum of money or funds.
37 (66) "Proposal" means a record signed by a secured party that

39 collateral in full or partial satisfaction of the obligation it secures
40 pursuant to IC 26-1-9.1-620, IC 26-1-9.1-621, and
41 IC 26-1-9.1-622.
42 (67) "Public-finance transaction" means a secured transaction in
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

253
1 connection with which:
2 (A) debt securities are issued;

5 (C) the debtor, obligor, secured party, account debtor, or other

11 (A) a record consisting of the record initially filed with or

29 default or other event not within the secured party's control has
30 relieved or may relieve the secured party from its obligation.
31 (70) "Record", except as used in "for record", "of record", "record

41 statute of the state governing business trusts requires that the

11 SECTION 225. IC 36-1-20-3.6 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
12 CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
13 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 3.6. (a) Except as provided in
14 subsection (c), a unit may not adopt or enforce an ordinance,
15 resolution, regulation, policy, or rule that:
16 (1) prohibits or restricts an owner of a privately owned
17 residential property from using the property as a rental
18 property; or
19 (2) has the effect of prohibiting or restricting the use of
20 property as a rental property.
21 (b) This section does not prohibit a unit from enforcing any:
22 (1) generally applicable health and safety regulations;
23 (2) building codes, fire codes, or reasonable occupancy
24 standards; or
25 (3) registration or inspection requirements set forth in this
26 chapter, provided the requirements do not operate to impose
27 a cap or limit described in subsection (a).
28 (c) A unit that has adopted an ordinance, resolution, regulation,
29 policy, or rule before January 1, 2026, that does not comply with
30 subsection (a) is exempt from the provisions of this section until
31 January 1, 2028, at which time a unit described in this subsection
32 shall comply with this section.

30 (1) day before the meeting, the notice in accordance with IC 5-3-1-4.

3 the amount necessary for other purposes described in
4 subdivision (4).
5 (B) Provide a written notice to the county auditor, the fiscal
6 body of the county or municipality that established the
7 department of redevelopment, and the officers who are
8 authorized to fix budgets, tax rates, and tax levies under
9 IC 6-1.1-17-5 for each of the other taxing units that is wholly
10 or partly located within the allocation area. The county auditor,
11 upon receiving the notice, shall forward this notice (in an
12 electronic format) to the department of local government
13 finance not later than June 15 of each year. The notice must:
14 (i) state the amount, if any, of excess assessed value that the
15 commission has determined may be allocated to the
16 respective taxing units in the manner prescribed in
17 subdivision (1); or
18 (ii) state that the commission has determined that there is no
19 excess assessed value that may be allocated to the respective
20 taxing units in the manner prescribed in subdivision (1).
21 The county auditor shall allocate to the respective taxing units
22 the amount, if any, of excess assessed value determined by the
23 commission. The commission may not authorize an allocation
24 of assessed value to the respective taxing units under this
25 subdivision if to do so would endanger the interests of the
26 holders of bonds described in subdivision (4) or lessors under
27 section 25.3 of this chapter. If a commission fails to provide
28 the notice under this clause, the county auditor shall
29 allocate five percent (5%) of the assessed value in the
30 allocation area that is used to calculate the allocation and
31 distribution of allocated tax proceeds under this section to
32 the respective taxing units. However, if the commission
33 notifies the county auditor and the department of local
34 government finance, no later than July 1, that it is unable
35 to meet its debt service obligations with regard to the
36 allocation area without all or part of the allocated tax
37 proceeds attributed to the assessed value that has been
38 allocated to the respective taxing units, then the county
39 auditor may not allocate five percent (5%) of the assessed
40 value in the allocation area that is used to calculate the
41 allocation and distribution of allocated tax proceeds under
42 this section to the respective taxing units.

32 of the enterprise zone that is within the allocation area as compared to
33 all such current property tax proceeds derived from the allocation area.

1 designed to benefit residents and employers in the enterprise zone or

17 The special fund may not be used for operating expenses of the
18 commission.
19 (4) Before June 15 of each year, the commission shall do the
20 following:
21 (A) Determine the amount, if any, by which the assessed value
22 of the taxable property in the allocation area for the most
23 recent assessment date minus the base assessed value, when
24 multiplied by the estimated tax rate of the allocation area, will
25 exceed the amount of assessed value needed to provide the
26 property taxes necessary to make, when due, principal and
27 interest payments on bonds described in subdivision (3) plus
28 the amount necessary for other purposes described in
29 subdivision (3) and subsection (g).
30 (B) Provide a written notice to the county auditor, the fiscal
31 body of the county or municipality that established the
32 department of redevelopment, the officers who are authorized
33 to fix budgets, tax rates, and tax levies under IC 6-1.1-17-5 for
34 each of the other taxing units that is wholly or partly located
35 within the allocation area, and (in an electronic format) the
36 department of local government finance. The notice must:
37 (i) state the amount, if any, of excess assessed value that the
38 commission has determined may be allocated to the
39 respective taxing units in the manner prescribed in
40 subdivision (1); or
41 (ii) state that the commission has determined that there is no
42 excess assessed value that may be allocated to the respective
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

314
1 taxing units in the manner prescribed in subdivision (1).
2 The county auditor shall allocate to the respective taxing units
3 the amount, if any, of excess assessed value determined by the
4 commission. The commission may not authorize an allocation
5 to the respective taxing units under this subdivision if to do so
6 would endanger the interests of the holders of bonds described
7 in subdivision (3). If a commission fails to provide the
8 notice under this clause, the county auditor shall allocate
9 five percent (5%) of the assessed value in the allocation
10 area that is used to calculate the allocation and distribution
11 of allocated tax proceeds under this section to the
12 respective taxing units. However, if the commission notifies
13 the county auditor and the department of local government
14 finance, no later than July 1, that it is unable to meet its
15 debt service obligations with regard to the allocation area
16 without all or part of the allocated tax proceeds attributed
17 to the assessed value that has been allocated to the
18 respective taxing units, then the county auditor may not
19 allocate five percent (5%) of the assessed value in the
20 allocation area that is used to calculate the allocation and
21 distribution of allocated tax proceeds under this section to
22 the respective taxing units.
23 (c) For the purpose of allocating taxes levied by or for any taxing
24 unit or units, the assessed value of taxable property in a territory in the
25 allocation area that is annexed by any taxing unit after the effective
26 date of the allocation provision of the resolution is the lesser of:
27 (1) the assessed value of the property for the assessment date with
28 respect to which the allocation and distribution is made; or
29 (2) the base assessed value.
30 (d) Property tax proceeds allocable to the redevelopment district
31 under subsection (b)(3) may, subject to subsection (b)(4), be
32 irrevocably pledged by the redevelopment district for payment as set
33 forth in subsection (b)(3).
34 (e) Notwithstanding any other law, each assessor shall, upon
35 petition of the commission, reassess the taxable property situated upon
36 or in, or added to, the allocation area, effective on the next assessment
37 date after the petition.
38 (f) Notwithstanding any other law, the assessed value of all taxable
39 property in the allocation area, for purposes of tax limitation, property
40 tax replacement, and formulation of the budget, tax rate, and tax levy
41 for each political subdivision in which the property is located, is the
42 lesser of:
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

315
1 (1) the assessed value of the property as valued without regard to
2 this section; or
3 (2) the base assessed value.
4 (g) If any part of the allocation area is located in an enterprise zone
5 created under IC 5-28-15, the unit that designated the allocation area
6 shall create funds as specified in this subsection. A unit that has
7 obligations, bonds, or leases payable from allocated tax proceeds under
8 subsection (b)(3) shall establish an allocation fund for the purposes
9 specified in subsection (b)(3) and a special zone fund. Such a unit
10 shall, until the end of the enterprise zone phase out period, deposit each
11 year in the special zone fund the amount in the allocation fund derived
12 from property tax proceeds in excess of those described in subsection
13 (b)(1) and (b)(2) from property located in the enterprise zone that
14 exceeds the amount sufficient for the purposes specified in subsection
15 (b)(3) for the year. A unit that has no obligations, bonds, or leases
16 payable from allocated tax proceeds under subsection (b)(3) shall
17 establish a special zone fund and deposit all the property tax proceeds
18 in excess of those described in subsection (b)(1) and (b)(2) in the fund
19 derived from property tax proceeds in excess of those described in
20 subsection (b)(1) and (b)(2) from property located in the enterprise
21 zone. The unit that creates the special zone fund shall use the fund,
22 based on the recommendations of the urban enterprise association, for
23 one (1) or more of the following purposes:
24 (1) To pay for programs in job training, job enrichment, and basic
25 skill development designed to benefit residents and employers in
26 the enterprise zone. The programs must reserve at least one-half
27 (1/2) of the enrollment in any session for residents of the
28 enterprise zone.
29 (2) To make loans and grants for the purpose of stimulating
30 business activity in the enterprise zone or providing employment
31 for enterprise zone residents in an enterprise zone. These loans
32 and grants may be made to the following:
33 (A) Businesses operating in the enterprise zone.
34 (B) Businesses that will move their operations to the enterprise
35 zone if such a loan or grant is made.
36 (3) To provide funds to carry out other purposes specified in
37 subsection (b)(3). However, where reference is made in
38 subsection (b)(3) to the allocation area, the reference refers, for
39 purposes of payments from the special zone fund, only to that part
40 of the allocation area that is also located in the enterprise zone.

42 finance shall make the rules and prescribe the forms and procedures

15 county auditor may not allocate five percent (5%) of the assessed
16 value in the allocation area that is used to calculate the allocation
17 and distribution of allocated tax proceeds under this section to the
18 respective taxing units.

1 finally determined for any subsequent assessment date; plus

7 (3) "Property taxes" means taxes imposed under IC 6-1.1 on real
8 property.
9 (b) A declaratory resolution adopted under section 16 of this chapter
10 before the date set forth in IC 36-7-14-39(b) pertaining to declaratory
11 resolutions adopted under IC 36-7-14-15 may include a provision with
12 respect to the allocation and distribution of property taxes for the
13 purposes and in the manner provided in this section. A declaratory
14 resolution previously adopted may include an allocation provision by
15 the amendment of that declaratory resolution in accordance with the
16 procedures set forth in section 18 of this chapter. The allocation
17 provision may apply to all or part of the military base development
18 area. The allocation provision must require that any property taxes
19 subsequently levied by or for the benefit of any public body entitled to
20 a distribution of property taxes on taxable property in the allocation
21 area be allocated and distributed as follows:
22 (1) Except as otherwise provided in this section, the proceeds of
23 the taxes attributable to the lesser of:
24 (A) the assessed value of the property for the assessment date
25 with respect to which the allocation and distribution is made;
26 or
27 (B) the base assessed value;
28 shall be allocated to and, when collected, paid into the funds of
29 the respective taxing units.
30 (2) The excess of the proceeds of the property taxes imposed for
31 the assessment date with respect to which the allocation and
32 distribution is made that are attributable to taxes imposed after
33 being approved by the voters in a referendum or local public
34 question conducted after April 30, 2010, not otherwise included
35 in subdivision (1) shall be allocated to and, when collected, paid
36 into the funds of the taxing unit for which the referendum or local
37 public question was conducted.
38 (3) Except as otherwise provided in this section, property tax
39 proceeds in excess of those described in subdivisions (1) and (2)
40 shall be allocated to the development authority and, when
41 collected, paid into an allocation fund for that allocation area that
42 may be used by the development authority and only to do one (1)
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

340
1 or more of the following:
2 (A) Pay the principal of and interest and redemption premium
3 on any obligations incurred by the development authority or
4 any other entity for the purpose of financing or refinancing
5 military base development or reuse activities in or directly
6 serving or benefiting that allocation area.
7 (B) Establish, augment, or restore the debt service reserve for
8 bonds payable solely or in part from allocated tax proceeds in
9 that allocation area or from other revenues of the development
10 authority, including lease rental revenues.
11 (C) Make payments on leases payable solely or in part from
12 allocated tax proceeds in that allocation area.
13 (D) Reimburse any other governmental body for expenditures
14 made for local public improvements (or structures) in or
15 directly serving or benefiting that allocation area.
16 (E) For property taxes first due and payable before 2009, pay
17 all or a part of a property tax replacement credit to taxpayers
18 in an allocation area as determined by the development
19 authority. This credit equals the amount determined under the
20 following STEPS for each taxpayer in a taxing district (as
21 defined in IC 6-1.1-1-20) that contains all or part of the
22 allocation area:
23 STEP ONE: Determine that part of the sum of the amounts
24 under IC 6-1.1-21-2(g)(1)(A), IC 6-1.1-21-2(g)(2),
25 IC 6-1.1-21-2(g)(3), IC 6-1.1-21-2(g)(4), and
26 IC 6-1.1-21-2(g)(5) (before their repeal) that is attributable to
27 the taxing district.
28 STEP TWO: Divide:
29 (i) that part of each county's eligible property tax
30 replacement amount (as defined in IC 6-1.1-21-2 (before its
31 repeal)) for that year as determined under IC 6-1.1-21-4
32 (before its repeal) that is attributable to the taxing district;
33 by
34 (ii) the STEP ONE sum.
35 STEP THREE: Multiply:
36 (i) the STEP TWO quotient; by
37 (ii) the total amount of the taxpayer's taxes (as defined in
38 IC 6-1.1-21-2 (before its repeal)) levied in the taxing district
39 that have been allocated during that year to an allocation
40 fund under this section.
41 If not all the taxpayers in an allocation area receive the credit
42 in full, each taxpayer in the allocation area is entitled to
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

341
1 receive the same proportion of the credit. A taxpayer may not
2 receive a credit under this section and a credit under section
3 32 of this chapter (before its repeal) in the same year.
4 (F) Pay expenses incurred by the development authority for
5 local public improvements or structures that were in the
6 allocation area or directly serving or benefiting the allocation
7 area.
8 (G) Reimburse public and private entities for expenses
9 incurred in training employees of industrial facilities that are
10 located:
11 (i) in the allocation area; and
12 (ii) on a parcel of real property that has been classified as
13 industrial property under the rules of the department of local
14 government finance.
15 However, the total amount of money spent for this purpose in
16 any year may not exceed the total amount of money in the
17 allocation fund that is attributable to property taxes paid by the
18 industrial facilities described in this clause. The
19 reimbursements under this clause must be made not more than
20 three (3) years after the date on which the investments that are
21 the basis for the increment financing are made.
22 (H) Expend money and provide financial assistance as
23 authorized in section 15(26) of this chapter.
24 The allocation fund may not be used for operating expenses of the
25 development authority.
26 (4) Except as provided in subsection (g), before July 15 of each
27 year the development authority shall do the following:
28 (A) Determine the amount, if any, by which property taxes
29 payable to the allocation fund in the following year will exceed
30 the amount of property taxes necessary to make, when due,
31 principal and interest payments on bonds described in
32 subdivision (3) plus the amount necessary for other purposes
33 described in subdivisions (2) and (3).

35 and the fiscal bodies and other officers who are authorized to
36 fix budgets, tax rates, and tax levies under IC 6-1.1-17-5 for
37 each of the other taxing units that is wholly or partly located
38 within the allocation area. The notice must:
39 (i) state the amount, if any, of the excess property taxes that
40 the development authority has determined may be paid to
41 the respective taxing units in the manner prescribed in
42 subdivision (1); or

342
1 (ii) state that the development authority has determined that
2 there is no excess assessed value that may be allocated to the
3 respective taxing units in the manner prescribed in
4 subdivision (1).

6 the amount, if any, of excess assessed value determined by the
7 development authority. The development authority may not
8 authorize a payment to the respective taxing units under this
9 subdivision if to do so would endanger the interest of the
10 holders of bonds described in subdivision (3) or lessors under
11 section 24 of this chapter. Property taxes received by a taxing
12 unit under this subdivision before 2009 are eligible for the
13 property tax replacement credit provided under IC 6-1.1-21
14 (before its repeal).

16 unit or units, the assessed value of taxable property in a territory in the
17 allocation area that is annexed by a taxing unit after the effective date
18 of the allocation provision of the declaratory resolution is the lesser of:
19 (1) the assessed value of the property for the assessment date with
20 respect to which the allocation and distribution is made; or
21 (2) the base assessed value.
22 (d) Property tax proceeds allocable to the military base development
23 district under subsection (b)(3) may, subject to subsection (b)(4), be
24 irrevocably pledged by the military base development district for
25 payment as set forth in subsection (b)(3).
26 (e) Notwithstanding any other law, each assessor shall, upon
27 petition of the development authority, reassess the taxable property
28 situated upon or in or added to the allocation area, effective on the next
29 assessment date after the petition.
30 (f) Notwithstanding any other law, the assessed value of all taxable
31 property in the allocation area, for purposes of tax limitation, property
32 tax replacement, and the making of the budget, tax rate, and tax levy
33 for each political subdivision in which the property is located is the
34 lesser of:
35 (1) the assessed value of the property as valued without regard to
36 this section; or
37 (2) the base assessed value.

39 created under IC 5-28-15, the development authority shall create funds
40 as specified in this subsection. A development authority that has

42 subsection (b)(3) shall establish an allocation fund for the purposes

1 specified in subsection (b)(3) and a special zone fund. The

3 out period, deposit each year in the special zone fund any amount in the

5 described in subsection (b)(1) and (b)(2) from property located in the
6 enterprise zone that exceeds the amount sufficient for the purposes
7 specified in subsection (b)(3) for the year. The amount sufficient for
8 purposes specified in subsection (b)(3) for the year shall be determined
9 based on the pro rata part of such current property tax proceeds from
10 the part of the enterprise zone that is within the allocation area as
11 compared to all such current property tax proceeds derived from the
12 allocation area. A development authority that does not have
13 obligations, bonds, or leases payable from allocated tax proceeds under
14 subsection (b)(3) shall establish a special zone fund and deposit all the
15 property tax proceeds in excess of those described in subsection (b)(1)
16 and (b)(2) that are derived from property in the enterprise zone in the
17 fund. The development authority that creates the special zone fund
18 shall use the fund (based on the recommendations of the urban
19 enterprise association) for programs in job training, job enrichment,
20 and basic skill development that are designed to benefit residents and
21 employers in the enterprise zone or for other purposes specified in
22 subsection (b)(3), except that where reference is made in subsection
23 (b)(3) to an allocation area it shall refer for purposes of payments from
24 the special zone fund only to that part of the allocation area that is also
25 located in the enterprise zone. The programs shall reserve at least
26 one-half (1/2) of their enrollment in any session for residents of the
27 enterprise zone.

30 local government finance county auditor shall, on forms prescribed
31 by the department of local government finance, adjust the base

1 to the military base development district under subsection (b)(3) than

7 prescribed by the department of local government finance, submit

21 establishment of the allocation area or the determination to issue bonds

24 property subject to the two percent (2%) tax cap, that is located in
25 the tax increment financing area and is excluded from the base
26 assessed value, shall upon completion of construction enter into a
27 written agreement with the development authority indicating the
28 owner shall be obligated to pay the property tax for the portion of
29 outstanding bonds in the tax increment financing district
30 attributable to the property until the term length of the original
31 outstanding bond is retired. The written agreement with the
32 development authority shall be considered a lien on the property
33 and shall be included as part of the residential real estate sales
34 disclosure under IC 32-21-5. If the property is subsequently sold as
35 a homestead property and becomes subject to the one percent (1%)
36 tax cap, the new owner shall be responsible for the lien on the
37 property attributable to the written agreement with the
38 development authority, and the new homestead property owner
39 shall be obligated to fulfill the terms of the written agreement
40 including the payment of the property tax liability included in the
41 agreement.

5 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2026 (RETROACTIVE)]:
6 Sec. 16. (a) All the real property within a fire protection district
7 constitutes a taxing district for the purpose of levying taxes to pay for
8 the construction, operation, and maintenance of district programs and
9 facilities. A tax levied must be levied at a uniform rate upon all taxable
10 property within the district. A fire protection district is a municipal
11 corporation within the meaning of the Constitution of Indiana and all
12 general statutes.

14 established by an ordinance or a resolution adopted under this
15 chapter after December 31, 2025. The district may not impose a tax
16 rate on the taxable property within the district that exceeds forty
17 cents ($0.40) per one hundred dollars ($100) of assessed valuation.

1 the property tax levy imposed for the territory. The part of the property
2 tax levy imposed for the territory for a particular year that shall be
3 attributed to a participating unit is equal to the amount determined in
4 the following STEPS:
5 STEP ONE: Determine the total amount of all property taxes
6 imposed by the participating unit in the year before the year in
7 which a property tax levy was first imposed for the territory.
8 STEP TWO: Determine the sum of the STEP ONE amounts for
9 all participating units.
10 STEP THREE: Divide the STEP ONE result by the STEP TWO
11 result.
12 STEP FOUR: Multiply the STEP THREE result by the property
13 tax levy imposed for the territory for the particular year.
14 (c) This subsection applies to a determination under subsection (b)
15 made in calendar years 2018, 2019, and 2020. The department of local
16 government finance may, for distributions made in calendar year 2022,
17 adjust the allocation amount determined under subsection (b) to correct
18 for any clerical or mathematical errors made in any determination for
19 calendar year 2018, 2019, or 2020, as applicable, including the
20 allocation amount for any taxing unit whose distribution was affected
21 by the clerical or mathematical error in those years. The department of
22 local government finance may apply the adjustment to the allocation
23 amount for a taxing unit over a period not to exceed ten (10) years in
24 order to offset the effect of the adjustment on the distribution.
25 (d) This subsection applies to a territory established by an ordinance
26 or a resolution adopted under this chapter after December 31, 2024.
27 Before additional revenue from a local income tax rate may be
28 allocated to the provider unit of a new territory due to an increased
29 property tax levy resulting from the establishment of the territory, the
30 county fiscal body must adopt an ordinance or resolution approving the
31 allocation.

41 (1) The ordinance or resolution is identical to the ordinances and

EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

365
1 section.
2 (2) Before adopting the ordinance or resolution, each participating
3 unit must comply with the notice and hearing requirements of
4 IC 6-1.1-41-3.
5 (3) The ordinance or resolution authorizes the provider unit to
6 establish the fund.
7 (4) The ordinance or resolution includes at least the following:
8 (A) The name of each participating unit and the provider unit.
9 (B) An agreement to impose a uniform tax rate upon all of the
10 taxable property within the territory for the equipment
11 replacement fund.
12 (C) The contents of the agreement to establish the fund.
13 An ordinance or a resolution adopted under this section takes effect as
14 provided in IC 6-1.1-41.
15 (b) If a fund is established, the participating units may agree to:
16 (1) impose a property tax to provide for the accumulation of
17 money in the fund to purchase fire protection equipment;
18 (2) incur debt to purchase fire protection equipment and impose
19 a property tax to retire the loan; or
20 (3) transfer an amount from the fire protection territory fund to
21 the fire equipment replacement fund not to exceed five percent
22 (5%) of the levy for the fire protection territory fund for that year;
23 or any combination of these options.

25 considered part of the maximum permissible ad valorem property
26 tax levy and may not exceed three and thirty-three hundredths cents
27 ($0.0333) per one hundred dollars ($100) of assessed value. Before
28 debt may be incurred, the fiscal body of a participating unit must adopt
29 an ordinance (in the case of a county or municipality) or a resolution
30 (in the case of a township or fire protection district) that specifies the
31 amount and purpose of the debt. The ordinance or resolution must be
32 identical to the other ordinances and resolutions adopted by the
33 participating units. Except as provided in subsection (d), if debt is to be
34 incurred for the purposes of a fund, the provider unit shall negotiate for
35 and hold the debt on behalf of the territory. However, the participating
36 units and the provider unit of the territory are jointly liable for any debt
37 incurred by the provider unit for the purposes of the fund. The most
38 recent adjusted value of taxable property for the entire territory must be
39 used to determine the debt limit under IC 36-1-15-6. A provider unit
40 shall comply with all general statutes and rules relating to the
41 incurrence of debt under this subsection.

23 to finance that construction or reconstruction.
24 (b) A loan obtained under this section:
25 (1) must have a fixed or variable interest rate;
26 (2) must mature within six (6) eleven (11) years after the day it is
27 obtained;
28 (3) shall be repaid from installments collected from assessments
29 of landowners over a five (5) ten (10) year period;
30 (4) is not subject to the provisions of section 94 of this chapter
31 that concern interest; and
32 (5) is not subject to the penalty provisions under IC 6-1.1-37-10
33 if the installments are timely paid.
34 (c) A construction loan fund is established for each construction or
35 reconstruction project loan that the board and the county fiscal body
36 authorize under this section. A construction loan fund consists of all
37 payments received from the owners assessed for the construction or
38 reconstruction project and may be used only to repay the associated
39 loan. If money remains in a construction loan fund after the associated
40 loan is paid in full, the remaining money in the fund may be transferred
41 to the general drain improvement fund.
42 (d) A county auditor shall maintain a separate ledger sheet for each
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

368
1 construction loan fund established under subsection (c) and record on

4 reconstruction project.
5 (e) A county auditor shall deposit all payments of principal and
6 interest received from the owners assessed for a construction or
7 reconstruction project in the associated construction loan fund.
8 (f) The board shall determine whether interest on the loan is to be
9 a part of the final assessment under section 84(a) of this chapter.

11 loan may be charged back to the benefited landowner at a rate that is

1 district, the district is extended.

5 (g) A municipality that becomes part of a district by reason of the
6 extension of the district under this section may continue to establish,
7 maintain, and operate parks and other recreational facilities under any
8 other law. The parks and other recreational facilities shall be operated
9 by the municipality separate from the parks and other recreational
10 facilities under the jurisdiction of the board in the same manner as they
11 would be operated by the municipality if it was not within the district.
12 (h) The operation of separate parks or recreational facilities by a

14 the municipality to pay all taxes imposed on property within the
15 district.
16 (i) The legislative body of a municipality may elect that the separate
17 parks or other recreational facilities of the municipality be maintained
18 or operated as a part of the district by adopting a resolution or an
19 ordinance to that effect. The separate park or other recreational facility
20 comes under the jurisdiction of the board at the time specified in the
21 resolution or ordinance.
22 SECTION 273. [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2024
23 (RETROACTIVE)] (a) This SECTION applies notwithstanding
24 IC 6-1.1-10, IC 6-1.1-11, or any other law or administrative rule or
25 provision.
26 (b) This SECTION applies to assessment dates after December
27 31, 2023, and before January 1, 2026.
28 (c) As used in this SECTION, "eligible property" means any
29 real property:
30 (1) that is owned, occupied, and used by a taxpayer that:
31 (A) is exempt from federal income taxation under Section
32 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code; and
33 (B) has a mission focused on preserving Indiana
34 landmarks;
35 (2) that is used for one (1) or more of the purposes described
36 in IC 6-1.1-10-16;
37 (3) that is a parcel that:
38 (A) was transferred to the taxpayer before January 1,
39 2024; and
40 (B) is located in Vanderburgh County;
41 (4) on which property taxes were imposed for the 2024 and
42 2025 assessment dates; and
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

371
1 (5) that would have been eligible for an exemption under
2 IC 6-1.1-10-16 for the 2024 and 2025 assessment dates if an
3 exemption application had been properly and timely filed
4 under IC 6-1.1 for the property.
5 (d) Before September 1, 2026, the owner of eligible property
6 may file a property tax exemption application and supporting
7 documents claiming a property tax exemption under this
8 SECTION for the eligible property for the 2024 and 2025
9 assessment dates.
10 (e) A property tax exemption application filed as provided in
11 subsection (d) is considered to have been properly and timely filed
12 for each assessment date.
13 (f) The following apply if the owner of eligible property files a
14 property tax exemption application as provided in subsection (d):
15 (1) The property tax exemption for the eligible property shall
16 be allowed and granted for the applicable assessment date by
17 the county assessor and county auditor of the county in which
18 the eligible property is located.
19 (2) The owner of the eligible property is not required to pay
20 any property taxes, penalties, or interest with respect to the
21 eligible property for the applicable assessment date.
22 (g) The exemption allowed by this SECTION shall be applied
23 without the need for any further ruling or action by the county
24 assessor, the county auditor, or the county property tax assessment
25 board of appeals of the county in which the eligible property is
26 located or by the Indiana board of tax review.
27 (h) To the extent the owner of the eligible property has paid any
28 property taxes, penalties, or interest with respect to the eligible
29 property for an applicable date and to the extent that the eligible
30 property is exempt from taxation as provided in this SECTION,
31 the owner of the eligible property is entitled to a refund of the
32 amounts paid. The owner is not entitled to any interest on the
33 refund under IC 6-1.1 or any other law to the extent interest has
34 not been paid by or on behalf of the owner. Notwithstanding the
35 filing deadlines for a claim under IC 6-1.1-26, any claim for a
36 refund filed by the owner of eligible property under this SECTION
37 before September 1, 2026, is considered timely filed. The county
38 auditor shall pay the refund due under this SECTION in one (1)
39 installment.
40 (i) This SECTION expires June 30, 2027.
